name: AWS Certified Generative AI Developer – Professional (AIP-C01)
description: Professional-level certification for GenAI developers integrating foundation models into applications and business workflows on AWS. Validates ability to design RAG solutions, implement agentic AI, apply prompt engineering, optimize GenAI apps, and enforce security/governance. Target: 2+ years building production apps on AWS + 1 year hands-on GenAI. 65 scored questions + 10 unscored, 170 minutes, 750/1000 passing score.
exportIntroQuestions:
## About this document

This document contains practice exam questions related to the **AWS Certified Generative AI Developer - Professional (AIP-C01)** certification.

### Certification context
This certification validates expertise in:
- Integrating foundation models into enterprise applications and workflows
- Building production-ready AI solutions on AWS
- Implementing RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) architectures
- Developing autonomous AI agents (Agentic AI)
- Working with vector databases and semantic search
- Customizing and optimizing generative AI models

### Purpose
This document compiles practice exam questions for study and review. Each question includes:
- The complete original question statement from the practice exam
- Justification explaining the concept behind the correct answer and the errors in the incorrect alternatives.

### Learning objective
Serve as reference material for discussion and deeper understanding of concepts related to foundation model integration and GenAI architectures, facilitating learning through practical question analysis.
exportIntroTranscripts:
## About this document

This document contains technical summaries of lesson transcripts related to the **AWS Certified Generative AI Developer - Professional (AIP-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Organizes and synthesizes lesson content in a structured format for quick review and deeper understanding of the concepts presented.
exportIntroChat:
## About this document

This document contains AI study conversations related to the **AWS Certified Generative AI Developer - Professional (AIP-C01)** certification.

### Purpose
Records discussions and explanations generated during interactive AI study sessions, serving as complementary reference material.

domains.name: Foundation Model Integration, Data Management, and Compliance
domains.description: Domain 1 (31%): Task 1.1 Analyze requirements and design GenAI solutions. Task 1.2 Select and configure FMs (benchmarks, cross-region inference, lifecycle). Task 1.3 Implement data validation and processing pipelines for FM consumption. Task 1.4 Design and implement vector store solutions. Task 1.5 Design retrieval mechanisms for FM augmentation (RAG). Task 1.6 Implement prompt engineering strategies and governance.

domains.name: Implementation and Integration
domains.description: Domain 2 (26%): Task 2.1 Implement agentic AI solutions and tool integrations (Strands Agents, MCP, multi-agent systems). Task 2.2 Implement model deployment strategies (Lambda, Bedrock, SageMaker endpoints). Task 2.3 Design enterprise integration architectures. Task 2.4 Implement FM API integrations. Task 2.5 Implement application integration patterns and development tools.

domains.name: AI Safety, Security, and Governance
domains.description: Domain 3 (20%): Task 3.1 Implement input and output safety controls (guardrails, content moderation, hallucination reduction). Task 3.2 Implement data security and privacy controls (VPC, PII detection, data masking). Task 3.3 Implement AI governance and compliance mechanisms (model cards, data lineage). Task 3.4 Implement responsible AI principles.

domains.name: Operational Efficiency and Optimization for GenAI Applications
domains.description: Domain 4 (12%): Task 4.1 Implement cost optimization and resource efficiency (token management, caching, tiered models). Task 4.2 Optimize application performance (latency, retrieval, throughput). Task 4.3 Implement monitoring systems for GenAI applications.

domains.name: Testing, Validation, and Troubleshooting
domains.description: Domain 5 (11%): Task 5.1 Implement evaluation systems for GenAI (quality metrics, A/B testing, LLM-as-a-Judge, RAG evaluation, agent performance). Task 5.2 Troubleshoot GenAI applications (context overflow, API errors, latency, drift).
